codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature handoff/transcript and commit `ae68a630f8999a62eec523b99a49bec6203a1613` against prior failed commits `847ecd852d89328dc1ff7bb6e17fc7c4a8949f23` and `33f9ac23ddb36df2d2bc9eff18c73a67f2e5d5c4`. The rerun does not resolve the prior blocking findings: runtime integration coverage for `refreshAll`/`runScheduledBackfillIfNeeded` is still source-text scanning only, and strict no-backward semantics are still relaxed by negative epsilon allowance.

issues.description: The claimed runtime integration coverage remains a source-text check (`test_refreshAll_guardCondition_isPresent`) that reads `UsageAggregator.swift` and asserts string presence rather than executing `UsageAggregator.refreshAll()` with persistence success/failure paths. This does not satisfy the feature requirement to add true runtime integration tests for both scenarios.

Monotonic guard still allows backward movement via negative epsilon (`>= -monotonicEpsilon`), and this rerun increases that allowance to 1ms (`monotonicEpsilon = 0.001`). The feature and prior failure require strict no-backward progression while preserving equal-timestamp idempotency without reopening negative backward epsilon.
The test hardcodes an absolute developer-specific path (`/Users/dewclaw/Documents/Projects/BurnBar/...`), making it brittle across CI/workspaces.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true` without evidence of required startup context reads.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file `.../handoffs/2026-04-09T21-11-09-757Z__m4-fix-backfill-runtime-execution-coverage-and-strict-idempotency__1c91c316-bba4-423b-9def-9124a1ff399b.json` lists only test/log commands in `verification.commandsRun`; no command demonstrates reading `mission.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, or `.factory/library/user-testing.md` as required by `.factory/skills/token-accounting-worker/SKILL.md`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared architecture guidance remains switcher-focused and omits token-accounting/backfill runtime context used in this mission.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md documents multi-account switcher architecture (profile registry, launch adapters, switch orchestrator) rather than token-accounting/backfill/reconciliation flows, while this feature targets BackfillCursorStore/UsageAggregator semantics.

summary: Fail: the rerun commit did not add the required runtime execution tests and further relaxes backward monotonic enforcement with a larger negative epsilon allowance, so the two prior blocking failures are not resolved.
